_meta.description: Canonical ERP+MOM object inventory for the business contract authority layer.

objects.label: Inventory Balance Snapshots
objects.purpose: Provide read-only snapshot truth for inventory position by site, warehouse, lot, and lineage so KPI, audit, and exception review do not query mutable transaction tables directly.
invariants: Snapshot rows must preserve source lineage and snapshot time.
The projection is read-only and must not become the mutation authority.
Site, plant, and warehouse scope must remain explicit on every row.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is inventory.inventory_balance_snapshot.* while canonical naming remains analytics.inventory-balance-snapshots.
compatibilityNotes: This projection is intentionally read-only and must never be treated as the mutation authority for inventory truth.

objects.label: MES OEE Snapshots
objects.purpose: Provide read-only OEE projection by equipment and shift so performance review, loss analysis, and gate meetings use stable snapshot truth rather than recalculating from mutable execution events.
invariants: OEE snapshots must preserve equipment, shift, and lineage context.
The projection is read-only and must not become execution authority.
OEE components must remain reproducible from upstream execution data.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is mes_execution.mes_oee_snapshots.* while canonical naming remains analytics.mes-oee-snapshots.
compatibilityNotes: The snapshot remains read-only and should feed analytics, not replace execution truth.

objects.label: Plant Performance Snapshots
objects.purpose: Provide read-only plant KPI snapshots so lean review, management review, and continuous-improvement boards consume a stable multi-signal projection with preserved lineage.
invariants: Plant performance snapshots must preserve lineage to upstream KPI sources.
The projection is read-only and must not replace operational truth.
Plant and site scope must remain explicit on every snapshot row.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is bi_datawarehouse.plant_performance_snapshots.* while canonical naming remains analytics.plant-performance-snapshots.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy KPI naming remains for migration trace, but the canonical snapshot is a read-only analytics projection.

objects.label: Production BOM Snapshots
objects.purpose: Provide read-only BOM snapshot truth per production order so planning, costing, and traceability review can inspect the released material structure without re-querying mutable planning records.
invariants: BOM snapshot rows must preserve production-order lineage and snapshot time.
The projection is read-only and must not replace planning truth.
Plant and site scope must remain explicit on every snapshot row.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is production.production_order_bom_snapshot.* while canonical naming remains analytics.production-bom-snapshots.
compatibilityNotes: The BOM snapshot is a read-only projection and must not become planning or order-authority truth.

objects.label: Production Route Snapshots
objects.purpose: Provide read-only route snapshot truth per production order so scheduling, traceability, and execution analysis can inspect the released routing structure without depending on mutable route masters.
invariants: Route snapshot rows must preserve production-order lineage and snapshot time.
The projection is read-only and must not replace routing or execution truth.
Plant and site scope must remain explicit on every snapshot row.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is production.production_order_route_snapshot.* while canonical naming remains analytics.production-route-snapshots.
compatibilityNotes: The route snapshot is a read-only projection and must not replace route or execution authority.

objects.label: Customer Care Cases
objects.purpose: Control customer complaint, follow-up, escalation, and closure so service issues remain governed and traceable instead of disappearing into touchpoint notes.
invariants: Closed customer-care cases must retain resolution evidence.
Escalated cases must keep clear owner assignment.
Cases that require return, NCR, or CAPA must preserve downstream trace.
runtimeExposureNotes: The current runtime surface remains crm.crm_customer_touchpoints.* while the canonical business name is customer-care-cases.
compatibilityNotes: crm_customer_touchpoints is still the live storage/runtime owner. The authored package makes the business-case meaning explicit without requiring a data move.

objects.label: Customer Purchase Orders
objects.purpose: Capture customer commitment as governed demand so commercial promise, SO linkage, and downstream delivery remain traceable from the first accepted PO.
invariants: A customer PO cannot be confirmed without at least one linked sales order.
A customer PO cannot close while any linked sales order remains non-terminal.
A customer PO cannot cancel while linked sales orders are active.
runtimeExposureNotes: Canonical create/list/detail/transition endpoints exist under commercial_customer.customer_purchase_orders.*.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy sales order fields still carry customer PO references during compatibility migration.

objects.label: Quotations
objects.purpose: Control commercial estimation, review, offer release, and conversion so customer demand does not bypass governed quote truth before SO commitment.
invariants: A quotation cannot be sent without completed review evidence.
Only accepted quotations can convert into sales orders.
A converted quotation must preserve trace to the resulting sales order.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is sales.quotes.* while canonical naming remains commercial_customer.quotations.
compatibilityNotes: crm_quotes_pipeline remains analytical or compatibility context and must not be treated as the lifecycle authority.

objects.label: Quote Effectiveness Reviews
objects.purpose: Capture structured quote win-loss and effectiveness analysis so commercial learning, margin review, and continuous improvement are preserved as governed assessment records instead of ad hoc notes.
invariants: Each review must reference a governed quotation.
Review evidence must preserve outcome and learning, not just a score.
Improvement recommendations should remain traceable when converted into follow-up actions.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is quality_lab.qual_effectiveness_reviews.* while canonical naming remains commercial_customer.quote-effectiveness-reviews.
compatibilityNotes: The assessment remains stored under quality-lab naming, but its business meaning belongs to the commercial quote-learning loop.

objects.label: Sales Orders
objects.purpose: Turn accepted commercial demand into governed revenue-producing execution demand spanning engineering, production release, shipment gate, and financial realization.
invariants: A sales order cannot enter production before commercial confirmation.
A sales order cannot ship before shipment gate controls pass.
A closed sales order must have terminal shipment evidence.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current runtime endpoints are sales.sales_orders.* while canonical naming remains commercial_customer.sales-orders.
compatibilityNotes: Status alias drift remains between legacy status-options and the governed order runtime. The package records the runtime truth explicitly.

objects.label: AP Invoices
objects.purpose: Control AP three-way match, hold, posting, payment, and close so procurement correction cannot bypass financial truth.
invariants: An AP invoice cannot post before three-way match.
An AP invoice cannot close before paid or voided state.
Backdated AP invoice changes require governed exception control.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current runtime endpoints are finance.ap_invoices.*.
compatibilityNotes: The split AP object supersedes the mixed AP/AR invoice legacy surface.

objects.label: AR Invoices
objects.purpose: Represent customer receivable truth from shipment or billing release through dispute and settlement, without depending on a mixed AP/AR legacy object.
invariants: An AR invoice must trace to sales-order or shipment truth.
An AR invoice cannot close before paid or cancelled state.
Disputed AR invoices must preserve customer-resolution trace.
runtimeExposureNotes: No dedicated AR invoice controller is active yet. The contract is explicit so downstream tooling stops inferring behavior from mixed AP/AR legacy tables.
compatibilityNotes: AR workflow truth is temporarily bridged from the mixed AP/AR status set until the dedicated AR runtime surface is completed.

objects.label: Backdate Exceptions
objects.purpose: Control governed posting-date exceptions so backdated financial or logistics adjustments cannot bypass closed-period discipline.
invariants: An active backdate exception must be timeboxed and signed.
The same subject and posting date must not receive duplicate active exceptions.
Closure, revocation, and expiry must preserve reason trace.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current runtime endpoints are exposed directly as /api/v1/finance/backdate-exceptions* through FinanceController.
compatibilityNotes: Backdate exceptions remain tied to period-close governance, but they are distinct first-class controls and must not collapse back into generic close metadata.

objects.label: Credit Memos
objects.purpose: Represent governed downward financial adjustments so AP and AR corrections remain explicit and auditable instead of hiding inside a mixed invoice object.
invariants: A credit memo requires positive amount, reason, and original invoice reference.
A credit memo must keep AP or AR scope explicit.
Credit memo creation must remain idempotent for retry safety.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current runtime endpoints are exposed directly as /api/v1/finance/credit-memos* through FinanceController.
compatibilityNotes: Credit memos are separated from the mixed AP/AR legacy invoice object so correction intent is no longer implicit.

objects.label: Debit Memos
objects.purpose: Represent governed upward financial adjustments so AP and AR correction exposure stays explicit, traceable, and separate from mixed invoice truth.
invariants: A debit memo requires positive amount, reason, and original invoice reference.
A debit memo must keep AP or AR scope explicit.
Debit memo creation must remain idempotent for retry safety.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current runtime endpoints are exposed directly as /api/v1/finance/debit-memos* through FinanceController.
compatibilityNotes: Debit memos are separated from the mixed AP/AR legacy invoice object so correction intent is no longer implicit.

objects.label: Fixed Asset Capitalizations
objects.purpose: Represent capitalization decisions that convert spend into governed asset value so maintenance, finance, and asset master stay aligned.
invariants: Capitalization must trace to asset identity and financial source.
Posted capitalization must not lose the link to AP or governed spend evidence.
Void and retirement actions must preserve audit trail.
objects.workflowSource: fin_fixed_assets asset-status bridge pending dedicated capitalization runtime
runtimeExposureNotes: No dedicated capitalization controller is active yet. The contract is explicit so downstream tooling stops inferring capitalization behavior from mixed asset records.
compatibilityNotes: Capitalization meaning is still partially bridged through fixed-asset records until a dedicated runtime surface is completed.

objects.label: Inventory Valuations
objects.purpose: Provide read-only inventory valuation projection per item, site, date, and valuation method so finance review and period close consume stable valuation truth with preserved lineage.
invariants: Inventory valuation rows must preserve source lineage and valuation context.
The projection is read-only and must not become transactional authority.
Valuation history must remain stable across period-close review.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is finance.inventory_valuations.* while canonical naming remains finance.inventory-valuations.
compatibilityNotes: This valuation object is a projection for finance review and must not replace transactional inventory truth.

objects.label: Period Closes
objects.purpose: Control accounting period close and reopen decisions so posting gates remain explicit, auditable, and separable from invoice records.
invariants: A period close control must be unique per period_code and ledger_scope.
Reopen requires a governed reason and preserved close history.
Close controls must retain electronic-signature evidence.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current runtime endpoints are exposed directly as /api/v1/finance/period-closes* through FinanceController.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy GL period-close naming remains only as compatibility trace while the explicit control object becomes system authority.

objects.label: Reconciliation Exceptions
objects.purpose: Control MES-ERP mismatch handling so interface drift is triaged, compensated, and closed instead of patched outside governed integration flow.
invariants: A reconciliation exception must preserve expected and actual payload trace.
Compensation must be governed and reviewable before closure.
Closed exceptions must retain recovery evidence.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is mes_execution.mes_erp_reconciliation_exceptions.* while canonical naming remains integration_resilience.reconciliation-exceptions.
compatibilityNotes: The runtime remains under the MES execution namespace, but the contract makes it explicit that this is an integration-control object.

objects.label: Customer Returns
objects.purpose: Control return authorization, receipt, evaluation, disposition, and closure so reverse-logistics truth stays linked to quality and finance outcomes.
invariants: A return cannot be physically received before authorization.
Returns that end in credit must retain finance linkage.
Closed returns must preserve disposition and quality trace.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is service_warranty.rma_orders.* while canonical naming remains inventory_logistics.customer-returns.
compatibilityNotes: rma_orders is the current storage/runtime owner. The package preserves reverse-logistics meaning without moving or renaming live data.

objects.label: Cycle Count Plans
objects.purpose: Control cycle-count scheduling and closure so stock accuracy is corrected through governed counting instead of ad hoc inventory adjustment.
invariants: A cycle-count plan must preserve warehouse and zone scope.
Completed plans must retain count-result and variance trace.
Count adjustments must not occur without governed count evidence.
objects.workflowSource: wms_cycle_count_plans status plus warehouse governance bridge
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is warehouse_management.wms_cycle_count_plans.* while canonical naming remains inventory_logistics.cycle-count-plans.
compatibilityNotes: Warehouse-management runtime naming remains active. The contract makes count-plan governance explicit for ERP/MOM orchestration.

objects.label: Freight Orders
objects.purpose: Controls freight execution from booking through delivery closure so shipment release, carrier handoff, and proof-of-delivery stay under governed logistics truth.
invariants: A freight order cannot move to booked without carrier commitment trace.
Delivered freight orders must retain proof-of-delivery evidence.
Closed freight orders must remain linked to governed shipment truth.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is transportation.freight_orders.* while canonical naming remains inventory_logistics.freight-orders.
compatibilityNotes: Transportation runtime naming remains the live API surface, but freight_orders is already the governed storage owner.

objects.label: Inventory Items
objects.purpose: Controls release, obsolescence, and inventory-usable truth for the shared item master without duplicating inventory item identity.
invariants: Active inventory items must retain released specification and revision trace.
Obsolete items must not remain eligible for new demand or purchasing release.
Item identity must remain stable across canonical and legacy runtime names.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface remains master_data.items.* while canonical naming remains inventory_logistics.inventory-items.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy items runtime still carries the operational surface. The canonical contract forbids identity fork between item_id and inventory_item_id naming.

objects.label: Inventory Movements
objects.purpose: Controls movement posting, hold, and reversal so inventory truth remains reconcilable under corrections and audit.
invariants: Posted inventory movements must preserve the source reference and stock effect trace.
Reversed inventory movements must retain governed correction evidence.
Held inventory movements must not silently remain unreconciled in downstream stock truth.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is inventory.inventory_transactions.* while canonical naming remains inventory_logistics.inventory-movements.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy inventory transaction naming remains the API surface. The canonical contract makes correction and reconciliation semantics explicit.

objects.label: Shipments
objects.purpose: Control outbound release, dispatch, delivery confirmation, and closure so OTIF and export-release truth stay governed from shipment creation to proof of delivery.
invariants: A shipment cannot release without shipment-gate clearance.
Delivered shipments must retain proof of delivery.
Cancelled shipments must not leave downstream finance or freight truth orphaned.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is shipping_compliance.shipments.* while canonical naming remains inventory_logistics.shipments.
compatibilityNotes: tms_shipments remains part of compatibility lineage, but shipments is the current business object to preserve for frontend/backend reasoning.

objects.label: Stock Balances
objects.purpose: Provide read-only consolidated stock balance truth by item and warehouse so planning, cycle counting, and valuation can consume stable balance projections without mutating source transactions.
invariants: Stock balance rows must preserve item and warehouse lineage.
The projection is read-only and must not replace inventory transaction truth.
Snapshot timing and source trace must remain explicit.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is inventory.stock_balances.* while canonical naming remains inventory_logistics.stock-balances.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy balance naming remains only for migration and traceability while the canonical stock-balance object remains a read-only projection.

objects.label: Warehouses
objects.purpose: Controls warehouse release, hold, and archive so blocked storage areas cannot silently remain active for receiving, picking, or shipment.
invariants: On-hold warehouses must not accept new receipt or picking activity without governed override.
Archived warehouses must not remain selectable for shipment or stock movement.
Warehouse identity must remain stable across storage and runtime surfaces.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is inventory.warehouses.* while canonical naming remains inventory_logistics.warehouses.
compatibilityNotes: Runtime naming is still inventory.warehouses.* even though the canonical contract sits under inventory_logistics.

objects.label: 5S Audits
objects.purpose: Controls 5S audit planning, execution, follow-up, verification, and closure so visual-factory discipline becomes a governed lean loop instead of a score-only snapshot.
invariants: Verified 5S audits must preserve verification evidence.
Closed 5S audits must not leave unowned findings open.
5S follow-up must remain traceable to the originating audit evidence.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is lean_manufacturing.lean_5s_audits.* while canonical naming remains maintenance_ehs.five-s-audits.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy 5S/workplace audit naming remains for compatibility, but lean_5s_audits is the governed storage/runtime owner.

objects.label: Incidents
objects.purpose: Control incident reporting, containment, investigation, corrective action, and closure so safety events cannot escape the EHS governance loop.
invariants: Closed incidents must preserve investigation and corrective-action evidence.
Obligation-linked incidents must retain regulatory trace.
Containment and corrective-action history must remain auditable.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is ehs_sustainability.ehs_incidents.* while canonical naming remains maintenance_ehs.incidents.
compatibilityNotes: The old incidents label remains for compatibility, but ehs_incidents is the governed storage/runtime owner.

objects.label: Maintenance Plans
objects.purpose: Controls preventive-maintenance plan approval, release, hold, and retirement so recurring PM cadence stays auditable before work orders are auto-released.
invariants: Active maintenance plans must reference released equipment and valid PM cadence.
On-hold plans must preserve the deferral reason and approver trace.
Retired plans must not continue auto-releasing maintenance work.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is plant_maintenance.pm_maintenance_plans.* while canonical naming remains maintenance_ehs.maintenance-plans.
compatibilityNotes: Plant-maintenance runtime naming remains the active API surface. The canonical contract keeps PM plan meaning explicit for ERP+MOM orchestration.

objects.label: Maintenance Work Orders
objects.purpose: Control preventive and corrective maintenance execution so equipment intervention, spare usage, and closeout stay governed instead of being hidden in note-only maintenance history.
invariants: A maintenance work order must reference equipment before release.
Completed maintenance work orders must preserve operation or closeout trace.
Cancelled work orders must preserve cancellation reason.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is plant_maintenance.pm_work_orders.* while canonical naming remains maintenance_ehs.maintenance-work-orders.
compatibilityNotes: Plant-maintenance runtime naming remains active while the canonical contract prevents maintenance execution from being confused with production work orders.

objects.label: Permits
objects.purpose: Controls permit submission, approval, activation, suspension, expiry, and closure so hazardous or regulated work cannot proceed outside a governed permit-to-work register.
invariants: Active permits must retain approved conditions and validity trace.
Expired or suspended permits must not remain work-enabling.
Closed permits must preserve completion or cancellation evidence.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is ehs_sustainability.ehs_permit_register.* while canonical naming remains maintenance_ehs.permits.
compatibilityNotes: Permit register runtime naming remains the live API surface. The canonical contract keeps permit-to-work meaning explicit across ERP+MOM orchestration.

objects.label: Safety Observations
objects.purpose: Controls safety good-catch and hazard observations through assignment, containment, and closure so frontline EHS risks cannot disappear into note-only reporting.
invariants: Assigned safety observations must preserve accountable ownership.
Closed safety observations must retain action trace or justified closure basis.
Observation-to-incident trace must remain auditable when the observation escalates or links to an incident.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is ehs_sustainability.safety_observations.* while canonical naming remains maintenance_ehs.safety-observations.
compatibilityNotes: Canonical naming is separated from the legacy runtime domain so AI and downstream tooling do not confuse EHS observations with note-only reporting.

objects.label: Assets
objects.purpose: Provide a canonical asset identity so finance, maintenance, and customer-installed asset references do not split into separate truths.
invariants: Asset identity must remain stable across finance, maintenance, and service contexts.
Disposed assets must preserve financial and operational lineage.
Retired assets must not remain available for new work or capitalization.
runtimeExposureNotes: No single native asset runtime surface is active yet. This package provides the canonical asset meaning across finance, maintenance, and service legacy surfaces.
compatibilityNotes: These legacy surfaces continue to exist for safe migration, but they must not compete as separate asset truths.

objects.label: Compliance Obligations
objects.purpose: Controls obligation due, waiver, and closure so permits, audits, and regulatory actions have a governed compliance anchor.
invariants: Overdue obligations must preserve owner accountability and action trace.
Waived obligations require approval evidence and must remain auditable.
Closed obligations must retain trace to their regulatory or audit source.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is quality_lab.qual_compliance_obligations.* while canonical naming remains master_data.compliance-obligations.
compatibilityNotes: Quality-lab runtime naming remains the live API surface. The canonical contract keeps obligation governance explicit across compliance, permit, and incident flows.

objects.label: Customers
objects.purpose: Controls customer commercial release eligibility, order acceptance, and evidence retention on the customer master.
invariants: Active customers must retain required commercial defaults and requirement trace.
On-hold customers must not accept new commitments without governed override.
Archived customers must not remain selectable for new quotes or orders.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is sales.customers.* while canonical naming remains master_data.customers.
compatibilityNotes: Sales runtime naming remains the active API surface. The canonical contract keeps customer-master meaning separate from downstream commercial documents.

objects.label: Employees
objects.purpose: Controls labor qualification, access eligibility, and employee release into production and compliance scopes.
invariants: Active employees must retain assignment and qualification evidence.
Suspended or terminated employees must not remain operationally eligible without governed override.
Employee identity must remain stable across HR and operational runtime surfaces.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is training_hr.employees.* while canonical naming remains master_data.employees.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy HCM naming remains for compatibility. The canonical contract prevents labor identity and eligibility rules from splitting across HR and operations.

objects.label: Equipment
objects.purpose: Controls equipment qualification, lockout, and usable-machine release before maintenance, calibration, and production execution.
invariants: Active equipment must retain qualification or commissioning basis.
Locked-out equipment must not remain available for dispatch or maintenance release without governed override.
Retired equipment must not continue feeding PM or execution release loops.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is calibration_equipment.equipment.* while canonical naming remains master_data.equipment.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy calibration and PM equipment naming remains for compatibility. The canonical contract prevents equipment identity and release rules from splitting across domains.

objects.label: Measuring Devices
objects.purpose: Provide a canonical measuring-device contract so calibration release, quarantine, and metrology evidence stop drifting between tool-control naming and legacy gauge terminology.
invariants: A measuring device must not be released without current calibration evidence.
Failed or expired measuring devices must remain quarantined until governed disposition.
Measurement-system studies must preserve the link back to the governed device identity.
objects.workflowSource: wave1_lifecycle_normalization shared tools status plus calibration evidence bridge
runtimeExposureNotes: No dedicated measuring-device API is active yet.
Runtime truth is partially bridged from calibration_equipment.tools.* plus calibration evidence records.
compatibilityNotes: The canonical measuring-device meaning is explicit even though current storage and runtime still piggyback on tooling control.

objects.label: Suppliers
objects.purpose: Controls supplier qualification and spend authorization before purchasing or receiving release.
invariants: Spend-authorized suppliers must retain qualification and commercial approval trace.
Blocked suppliers must not remain eligible for new procurement or receiving release.
Supplier identity must remain stable across vendor and canonical supplier naming.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface remains purchasing.vendors.* while canonical naming remains master_data.suppliers.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy vendor runtime naming remains for compatibility. The canonical contract prevents supplier-master meaning from splitting across sourcing and quality modules.

objects.label: Tools
objects.purpose: Control tool readiness, wear containment, and retirement before a tool can be issued to production, maintenance, or calibration use.
invariants: Only qualified tools may enter issue or in-use state.
Broken or retired tools must not remain available for new operations.
Tool condition history must remain auditable across regrind, issue, and return cycles.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is calibration_equipment.tools.* while canonical naming remains master_data.tools.
compatibilityNotes: Calibration/equipment runtime naming remains active, but the canonical contract keeps tool control separate from measuring-system control.

objects.label: Machine Alarms
objects.purpose: Capture machine-originated alarm and fault events with operator acknowledgement, escalation, downtime linkage, and maintenance follow-up without manually inventing machine data.
invariants: Alarm creation must carry machine, adapter, or edge-gateway source evidence.
Operator acknowledgement must link to the admin user/operator identity model.
Clearing or escalation must preserve reason and actor; alarm rows are never hard-deleted.
objects.workflowSource: workflow-library wf_mes_machine_alarm bridged through GenericCrud workflow-engine runtime
runtimeExposureNotes: Live runtime surface is mes_execution.mes_machine_alarms.*. Frontend may display and acknowledge only machine-originated records; it must not manually create fake machine alarms.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy alarm event names are aliases; mes_machine_alarms is the PostgreSQL machine runtime authority.

objects.label: Dispatch Lists
objects.purpose: Controls shopfloor dispatch release, hold, and execution closure so operations cannot start or disappear from queue outside the governed MES release horizon.
invariants: Released dispatch entries must retain a readiness snapshot for constraints and resource availability.
Completed dispatch entries must preserve execution trace before closure.
Cancelled dispatch entries must retain reason and upstream plan trace.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is mes_execution.mes_dispatch_queue.* while canonical naming remains planning_production.dispatch-lists.
compatibilityNotes: MES runtime naming remains the live API surface. The canonical contract keeps dispatch intent separated from raw queue mechanics.

objects.label: IPQC Inspections
objects.purpose: Control in-process quality checks so process escapes trigger hold, reinspection, or NCR before final inspection and shipment release.
invariants: Rejected IPQC results must preserve containment or NCR trace.
Closed IPQC records must retain result evidence.
Reinspection must reference the prior rejected inspection path.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is quality_management.ipqc_inspections.* while canonical naming remains planning_production.ipqc-inspections.
compatibilityNotes: The table name already matches the business object, but the runtime endpoint domain still sits under quality_management for compatibility.

objects.label: Job Orders
objects.purpose: Translate confirmed sales demand into governed production intent so materials, routing, and downstream WO dispatch stay synchronized with commercial commitment.
invariants: A job order cannot release without linked confirmed sales demand.
A job order cannot close while child work orders remain open.
Completed job orders must reflect reconciled execution totals.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current runtime endpoints are production.job_orders.* backed by the governed order store.
compatibilityNotes: Status aliasing still needs convergence between status registry and governed order runtime.

objects.label: Production Operations
objects.purpose: Controls operation-level execution, hold, completion, and scrap so genealogy and in-process quality can anchor to the actual operation step.
invariants: Production operations must remain subordinate to governed parent production execution records.
Quality-hold operations must preserve IPQC or NCR trace before release.
Completed or scrapped operations must retain execution totals and disposition evidence.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is production.job_operations.* while canonical naming remains planning_production.production-operations.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy operation tables remain for compatibility, but the canonical contract fixes the business meaning as a contained child under production execution.

objects.label: Production Plans
objects.purpose: Control factory plan review, publication, freeze, and completion so dispatch, capacity, and OTIF commitments all read the same released schedule truth.
invariants: A production plan cannot publish without completed review and approval.
Frozen plans require traceable override or controlled replanning.
Completed plans must reconcile to execution truth.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is production.production_schedule.* while canonical naming remains planning_production.production-plans.
compatibilityNotes: The current storage/runtime owner is production_schedule. The package makes the business-plan semantics explicit without moving data.

objects.label: Work Orders
objects.purpose: Control executable shopfloor release, execution, and quality hold at the operation level so production truth is governed where work is actually performed.
invariants: A work order cannot start before release.
Quality hold must interrupt normal execution truth.
A closed work order must retain execution evidence and downstream quality linkage.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current runtime endpoints are production.work_orders.* with wave1-governed transition semantics.
compatibilityNotes: This object already has aligned lifecycle metadata from wave1 and serves as the reference style for authored packages.

objects.label: IQC Inspections
objects.purpose: Gate inbound material before stock release so supplier quality, quarantine, and NCR escalation are recorded from a governed incoming inspection object.
invariants: Accepted incoming material must have inspection result evidence.
Rejected incoming material must preserve quarantine or NCR routing evidence.
Conditional release requires waiver approval evidence.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current runtime endpoints are quality_management.incoming_inspections.* and are used by the Purchasing workspace.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy IQC naming remains for compatibility, but incoming_inspections is the canonical object.

objects.label: Purchase Receipt Corrections
objects.purpose: Control governed correction and reversal after receipt, IQC, or invoice impact so partial completion can be reconciled through traceable recovery instead of manual patches.
invariants: A receipt correction must retain both the physical receipt trace and the financial impact trace.
A posted correction must not leave unreconciled inventory or AP side effects.
A voided correction must preserve the recovery reason and approval history.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is purchasing.purchase_receipt_corrections.* while canonical naming remains procurement_supplier_quality.purchase-receipt-corrections.
compatibilityNotes: The current purchasing namespace remains live for compatibility while canonical API rollout is still pending.

objects.label: Purchase Receipts
objects.purpose: Control physical receipt, quarantine, acceptance, putaway, and reversal so stock and AP matching cannot outrun receiving truth.
invariants: A receipt cannot put away material before IQC acceptance.
Quarantined receipts must not release uncontrolled stock.
Receipt reversals must preserve correction and AP impact trace.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is purchasing.purchase_receipts.* while canonical naming remains procurement_supplier_quality.purchase-receipts.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy goods-receipt naming remains for compatibility, but purchase_receipts is the canonical lifecycle owner.

objects.label: Purchase Requisitions
objects.purpose: Authorize buy demand before sourcing or PO release so procurement cannot bypass planning, budget, or gate controls.
invariants: A requisition cannot source before approval.
A requisition should trace back to governed production or inventory demand.
Cancelled requisitions must preserve cancellation reason and audit trail.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is purchasing.purchase_requisitions.* while canonical naming remains procurement_supplier_quality.purchase-requisitions.
compatibilityNotes: No data move is required; the authored package clarifies requisition intent while retaining the current purchasing runtime.

objects.label: Supplier ASNs
objects.purpose: Control inbound advance shipment notice, discrepancy handling, and receiving readiness so physical receipts cannot outrun supplier intent and dock planning truth.
invariants: An ASN must reference a governed supplier purchase order before confirmation.
A discrepant ASN must preserve discrepancy reason and downstream disposition trace.
An ASN cannot close without receipt or discrepancy completion trace.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is purchasing.supplier_asns.* while canonical naming remains procurement_supplier_quality.supplier-asns.
compatibilityNotes: The supplier_asns runtime name stays in place for compatibility until canonical API rollout is complete.

objects.label: Supplier Purchase Orders
objects.purpose: Authorize governed external spend and supplier commitment so receiving, IQC, and AP matching cannot bypass approved procurement intent.
invariants: A supplier PO cannot release before approval.
A supplier PO cannot close before governed receiving reaches terminal state.
A supplier PO must remain linked to an approved requisition or other governed demand source.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current workflow surface is the Purchasing workspace and purchasing.purchase_orders.* endpoints.
compatibilityNotes: A dedicated supplier_purchase_orders controller is still pending; the purchasing alias remains the live runtime surface.

objects.label: Audit Programs
objects.purpose: Control internal and supplier audit planning, execution, verification, and closure so compliance evidence and systemic findings remain governed from schedule through improvement follow-up.
invariants: An audit program must retain scope, owner, and objective before execution.
Closed audits must preserve finding and closeout evidence.
Systemic findings should trace to CAPA or improvement follow-up.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is quality_lab.qual_audit_programs.* while canonical naming remains quality_improvement.audit-programs.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy audit naming remains only as compatibility trace while the canonical business object stays audit-programs.

objects.label: Concessions
objects.purpose: Control temporary acceptance or shipment-release exception decisions so quality deviation from requirement cannot bypass governed approval and traceability.
invariants: A concession must preserve explicit scope, duration, and approval evidence.
A concession affecting shipment release must remain traceable to the related order or inspection.
Rejected concession requests must keep rejection reason for audit and learning.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is quality_management.concessions.* while canonical naming remains quality_improvement.concessions.
compatibilityNotes: The storage name stays the same, but the canonical domain is quality_improvement so concession control is treated as part of the governed quality-improvement loop.

objects.label: Control Plans
objects.purpose: Govern APQP/FMEA-linked control plans so product and process characteristics, control methods, reaction plans, approvals, revisions, and shop-floor inspection execution stay traceable.
invariants: An active control plan must have linked FMEA or approved reason for standalone use.
Control characteristics must exist before activation.
Archiving never deletes historical control-plan revisions or inspection linkage.
objects.workflowSource: workflow-library wf_fmea bridged through GenericCrud workflow-engine runtime
runtimeExposureNotes: Live runtime surface is fmea_apqp.control_plans.* through GenericCrud workflow-engine bridge; direct status mutation is not authoritative.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy FMEA/APQP names remain compatibility aliases; control_plans is the PostgreSQL authority.

objects.label: Corrective Actions
objects.purpose: Drive verified corrective and preventive action so recurring quality loss is closed through owned tasks, effectiveness review, and audit trace.
invariants: A CAPA requires root cause evidence before action planning.
A CAPA cannot close without effectiveness verification.
CAPA tasks must remain linked to the originating NCR, audit, or systemic signal.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current runtime endpoints are quality_management.capa_records.*.
compatibilityNotes: The short capa alias is retained only for compatibility and should not be treated as the canonical business name.

Training and document updates remain required outcomes for some CAPA actions, but they are not modeled yet as first-class canonical resources and therefore stay in evidence/work-instruction traces instead of upstream/downstream object references.

objects.label: Deviations
objects.purpose: Control temporary process, product, or documentation deviations so exception handling remains explicit, time-bounded, and auditable instead of disappearing inside generic status notes.
invariants: Deviation scope and risk must be explicit before review.
Closed deviations must preserve disposition, reason, and approval trace.
Systemic deviations should link to CAPA or equivalent follow-up.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is quality_management.deviations.* while canonical naming remains quality_improvement.deviations.
compatibilityNotes: Deviation naming is retained for migration safety, but the canonical contract clarifies that this is a governed exception object, not a generic workflow placeholder.

objects.label: FQC Inspections
objects.purpose: Preserve the final-quality-control vocabulary used by operations while resolving it onto the governed outgoing-inspection truth so frontend and AI tooling stop treating FQC as a separate storage authority.
invariants: FQC aliasing must not create split truth with OQC.
Rejected final inspection must retain NCR or concession linkage.
Shipment release requires accepted or governed waived final inspection.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface remains quality_management.oqc_inspections.* while FQC stays an alias label for final-stage outgoing inspection.
compatibilityNotes: FQC remains for language continuity only and must not become an independent storage or workflow authority.

objects.label: Improvement Actions
objects.purpose: Control lean improvement intake, approval, implementation, verification, and closure so kaizen and systemic improvement work produces auditable benefit instead of unmanaged ideas.
invariants: Approved improvements must retain owner, target KPI, and expected benefit.
Verified improvements must preserve before-and-after evidence or documented learning.
Closed improvements must keep trace back to the originating audit, NCR, or CI signal.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is audit_risk.improvement_projects.* while canonical naming remains quality_improvement.improvement-actions.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy improvement naming remains only as compatibility trace while the canonical business name is improvement-actions.

objects.label: MSA Studies
objects.purpose: Capture measurement-system-analysis and GRR evidence so acceptance of measurement capability is explicit before relying on data for release, SPC, or capability decisions.
invariants: MSA studies must preserve the protocol and result used to justify measurement capability.
Studies with unacceptable GRR should remain traceable to follow-up improvement or calibration action.
MSA evidence must remain attributable to the governed equipment or measuring-device context.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is calibration_equipment.calibration_grr_studies.* while canonical naming remains quality_improvement.msa-studies.
compatibilityNotes: GRR study runtime naming remains in the calibration domain, but the canonical business meaning is measurement-system analysis in the quality-improvement loop.

objects.label: Nonconformances
objects.purpose: Contain and disposition quality escapes from incoming, in-process, outgoing, or returned product so no defect signal disappears outside a governed NCR record.
invariants: An NCR must retain source trace back to the originating inspection, process, or return event.
An NCR cannot close without disposition evidence.
Systemic NCRs should link to CAPA when root cause requires corrective action.
runtimeExposureNotes: Current runtime endpoints are quality_management.ncr_records.*.
compatibilityNotes: The singular nonconformance alias is marked for archive isolation once all readers migrate.

objects.label: OQC Inspections
objects.purpose: Control outgoing quality release so finished goods cannot pass to shipment without governed final inspection truth.
invariants: Shipment release requires accepted OQC or governed waiver.
Rejected OQC must preserve NCR or concession trace.
Waived OQC requires approval evidence.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is quality_management.oqc_inspections.* while canonical naming remains quality_improvement.oqc-inspections.
compatibilityNotes: Legacy pass/fail/conditional phrasing remains in some older handlers and reports, but the canonical outgoing-gate truth is accepted/rejected/waived.

objects.label: Override Controls
objects.purpose: Govern temporary override, waiver, and release bypass decisions so exceptional shipments or process gates remain explicit, timeboxed, signed, and reviewable.
invariants: Only one active override may exist for the same governed gate subject and control code.
Revocation, expiry, and closure must retain reason trace.
Overrides must remain timeboxed and signed.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is /api/v1/governance/override-controls while canonical naming remains quality_improvement.override-controls.
compatibilityNotes: Shipment-gate override files remain compatibility history only; the governed business meaning is now explicit as override-controls.

objects.label: SPC Observations
objects.purpose: Preserve structured statistical-process-control observations so process capability, rule violations, and escalation triggers are governed assessment evidence instead of spreadsheet-only history.
invariants: Every SPC observation must keep process and sampling context.
Out-of-control signals should remain traceable to follow-up NCR or improvement action.
Capability and rule-evaluation evidence must remain reproducible from the recorded observation.
runtimeExposureNotes: The live runtime surface is quality_management.spc_data.* while canonical naming remains quality_improvement.spc-observations.
compatibilityNotes: The stored table name remains spc_data, but the canonical object clarifies that these rows are governed SPC observations in the quality loop.

objects.label: Lot Genealogy
objects.purpose: Provide forward and backward genealogy for lot, serial, batch, and production-order relationships so containment, recall, NCR, and customer-return decisions can be traced to source records.
invariants: Traceability corrections must be append-only or superseding, never destructive.
Forward and backward trace must use the same governed source tables.
Containment decisions must be able to cite the genealogy link evidence used.
objects.workflowSource: PostgreSQL traceability tables exposed through GenericCrud runtime
runtimeExposureNotes: Live storage surfaces are genealogy_link, trace_genealogy_batches, trace_genealogy_links and mes_part_genealogy; lot_genealogy is only the business-facing semantic name.
compatibilityNotes: lot_genealogy is not a physical table in the current PostgreSQL registry; it resolves to genealogy_link and trace_genealogy_* runtime tables.
